ch02.title: Degrees of Freedom (Grübler)
ch02.prompt:
Grübler's formula gives the DOF of a mechanism:

    dof = m·(N − 1 − J) + Σ fᵢ

m is the DOF of one rigid body (3 planar, 6 spatial), N is the number of links *including ground*, J the number of joints, and `fs` lists each joint's freedoms.

Implement `grubler(m, N, J, fs)`.
ch02.starter:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    pass
ch02.solution:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    return m * (N - 1 - J) + sum(fs)

ch02.title: Spatial Open-Chain DOF
ch02.prompt: An open chain of N links (incl. ground) joined by N−1 single-DOF revolute joints in space (m=6) has exactly N−1 DOF. Use the same `grubler(m, N, J, fs)` to confirm it.
ch02.starter:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    pass
ch02.solution:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    return m * (N - 1 - J) + sum(fs)

ch02.title: Degree of Redundancy
ch02.prompt:
A robot with `dof` joints performing a task that needs `task` dimensions has redundancy = dof − task (when positive, infinitely many joint solutions reach a goal).

Implement `redundancy(dof, task)`.
ch02.solution:
def redundancy(dof, task):
    return dof - task

ch02.title: DOF of Free Rigid Bodies
ch02.prompt: n unconstrained rigid bodies each have m DOF, so together m·n. Implement `free_bodies_dof(m, n)`.
ch02.solution:
def free_bodies_dof(m, n):
    return m * n

ch03.title: Vector from [ω] (so3→vec)
ch03.prompt:
Recover ω from a skew-symmetric matrix: ω = (M₂₁, M₀₂, M₁₀).

Implement `so3_to_vec(M)` (3×3 list → 3-vector).

ch03.title: Inverse of a Rotation Matrix
ch03.prompt: For R ∈ SO(3), R⁻¹ = Rᵀ. Implement `rot_inv(R)` returning the transpose of the 3×3 matrix.
ch03.solution:
def rot_inv(R):
    return [[R[j][i] for j in range(3)] for i in range(3)]

ch03.title: Planar Rotation Matrix
ch03.prompt:
The 2×2 rotation by angle θ is [[cosθ,−sinθ],[sinθ,cosθ]].

Implement `rot2(t)`.
ch03.solution:
import math

def rot2(t):
    c, s = math.cos(t), math.sin(t)
    return [[c, -s], [s, c]]

ch03.title: Rotation about ẑ (Rz)
ch03.solution:
import math

def rotz(t):
    c, s = math.cos(t), math.sin(t)
    return [[c,-s,0],[s,c,0],[0,0,1]]

ch03.title: 3×3 Matrix Multiply
ch03.prompt: Composing rotations is matrix multiplication. Implement `matmul3(A, B)` for two 3×3 matrices (no numpy).

ch03.title: Rotation Angle from R
ch03.prompt:
The exponential-coordinate angle of R ∈ SO(3) is θ = arccos((tr R − 1)/2). Clamp the argument to [−1,1].

Implement `rot_angle(R)`.

ch04.title: Inverse of a Homogeneous Transform
ch04.prompt:
T = [[R,p],[0,1]] ∈ SE(3) has inverse [[Rᵀ,−Rᵀp],[0,1]].

Implement `trans_inv(T)` for a 4×4 list.
ch04.solution:
def trans_inv(T):
    R=[[T[i][j] for j in range(3)] for i in range(3)]
    p=[T[i][3] for i in range(3)]
    Rt=[[R[j][i] for j in range(3)] for i in range(3)]
    q=[-sum(Rt[i][k]*p[k] for k in range(3)) for i in range(3)]
    return [Rt[0]+[q[0]],Rt[1]+[q[1]],Rt[2]+[q[2]],[0,0,0,1]]

ch04.title: Build Transform from (R, p)
ch04.prompt:
Assemble T = [[R,p],[0,1]] from a 3×3 R and 3-vector p.

Implement `rp_to_trans(R, p)`.

ch04.title: Split Transform into (R, p)
ch04.prompt:
Extract the rotation and translation from a 4×4 T. Return `[R, p]` where R is 3×3 and p is a 3-vector.

Implement `trans_to_rp(T)`.
ch04.solution:
def trans_to_rp(T):
    R = [[T[i][j] for j in range(3)] for i in range(3)]
    p = [T[i][3] for i in range(3)]
    return [R, p]

ch04.title: Planar 2R Forward Kinematics
ch04.prompt:
Planar 2R arm: x = L1c1 + L2c12, y = L1s1 + L2s12 (c12 = cos(θ1+θ2)).

Implement `fk_2r(L1, L2, t1, t2)` → [x, y].

ch04.title: Planar 3R Forward Kinematics
ch04.prompt:
Planar 3R arm end-effector pose is [x, y, φ] with φ = θ1+θ2+θ3 and x,y the chained link tips.

Implement `fk_3r(L1, L2, L3, t1, t2, t3)`.
ch04.solution:
import math

def fk_3r(L1, L2, L3, t1, t2, t3):
    a, b, c = t1, t1+t2, t1+t2+t3
    x = L1*math.cos(a)+L2*math.cos(b)+L3*math.cos(c)
    y = L1*math.sin(a)+L2*math.sin(b)+L3*math.sin(c)
    return [x, y, c]

ch05.title: Planar 2R Jacobian
ch05.prompt:
J = [[−L1s1−L2s12, −L2s12],[L1c1+L2c12, L2c12]].

Implement `jacobian_2r(L1, L2, t1, t2)` (2×2).
ch05.solution:
import math

def jacobian_2r(L1, L2, t1, t2):
    s1,c1=math.sin(t1),math.cos(t1)
    s12,c12=math.sin(t1+t2),math.cos(t1+t2)
    return [[-L1*s1-L2*s12,-L2*s12],[L1*c1+L2*c12,L2*c12]]

ch05.title: Detect a Singularity
ch05.prompt: A 2×2 Jacobian is singular when |det J| < 1e-9. Implement `is_singular(J)` → bool.

ch05.title: Manipulability Measure
ch05.prompt:
For a square Jacobian, the manipulability μ = √(det(JJᵀ)) = |det J|.

Implement `manipulability(J)` for a 2×2 J.

ch05.title: Statics: Joint Torques τ = JᵀF
ch05.prompt:
Static equilibrium maps an end-effector wrench F to joint torques τ = Jᵀ F. For a 2×2 J and 2-vector F, return [τ1, τ2].

Implement `joint_torques(J, F)`.

ch06.title: IK: Elbow Angle
ch06.prompt: cosθ2 = (x²+y²−L1²−L2²)/(2L1L2); θ2 = arccos(clamp(cosθ2)). Implement `ik_2r_th2(L1, L2, x, y)`.

ch06.title: Is the Target Reachable?
ch06.prompt:
A planar 2R arm reaches (x,y) iff |L1−L2| ≤ √(x²+y²) ≤ L1+L2.

Implement `ik_reachable(L1, L2, x, y)` → bool.
ch06.solution:
import math

def ik_reachable(L1, L2, x, y):
    r=math.hypot(x,y)
    return abs(L1-L2)-1e-9 <= r <= L1+L2+1e-9

ch06.title: Law of Cosines (angle)
ch06.prompt:
Angle opposite side c in a triangle with sides a,b,c: γ = arccos((a²+b²−c²)/(2ab)).

Implement `law_of_cosines(a, b, c)`.

ch07.title: Stewart Platform Mobility
ch07.prompt: The 6-UPS Stewart platform: m=6, N=14 links, J=18 joints, each leg's joints contribute 2+1+3 freedoms. Use `grubler(m, N, J, fs)`; the result should be 6.
ch07.starter:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    pass
ch07.solution:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    return m * (N - 1 - J) + sum(fs)

ch07.title: Planar 3-RRR Parallel Robot
ch07.prompt: A planar 3-RRR parallel manipulator has m=3, N=8, J=9, all 1-DOF joints. Confirm dof = 3 with `grubler`.
ch07.starter:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    pass
ch07.solution:
def grubler(m, N, J, fs):
    return m * (N - 1 - J) + sum(fs)

ch08.title: Single-Joint Inverse Dynamics
ch08.prompt: A pendulum joint: τ = I·θ̈ + m·g·l·sinθ. Implement `joint_torque(I, ddth, m, g, l, th)`.

ch08.title: Kinetic Energy ½q̇ᵀM(q)q̇
ch08.prompt: With mass matrix M and joint velocity q̇, KE = ½ q̇ᵀ M q̇. Implement `kinetic_energy(M, v)` for n×n M and length-n v.

ch08.title: Rotational Kinetic Energy
ch08.prompt: Spinning body: KE = ½ I ω². Implement `rotational_ke(I, w)`.

ch09.title: Cubic Time Scaling
Cubic Scaling Velocity ṡ(t)
ch09.prompt: ṡ(t) = (6/Tf)·(t/Tf)·(1 − t/Tf). Implement `cubic_dot(Tf, t)`. (Peak speed is at the midpoint.)

ch09.title: Quintic Time Scaling
ch09.prompt:
s(t) = 10(t/Tf)³ − 15(t/Tf)⁴ + 6(t/Tf)⁵ (zero end velocity AND acceleration).

Implement `quintic(Tf, t)`.

ch09.title: Straight-Line Path Point
ch09.prompt: A straight-line path: X(s) = Xstart + s·(Xend − Xstart), elementwise. Implement `straight_line(start, end, s)` for equal-length vectors.
ch09.solution:
def straight_line(start, end, s):
    return [start[i] + s*(end[i]-start[i]) for i in range(len(start))]

ch10.title: Grid Neighbors (4-connected)
ch10.prompt:
Return the valid 4-connected neighbors of cell (r,c) inside an rows×cols grid, as a **sorted** list of [r,c].

Implement `grid_neighbors(r, c, rows, cols)`.
ch10.starter:
def grid_neighbors(r, c, rows, cols):
    pass

ch10.title: Manhattan Heuristic
ch10.prompt: A* on a 4-connected grid uses h = |Δr| + |Δc|. Implement `manhattan(a, b)` for cells [r,c].
ch10.solution:
def manhattan(a, b):
    return abs(a[0]-b[0]) + abs(a[1]-b[1])

ch10.title: Euclidean Distance
ch10.prompt: Straight-line heuristic h = √(Δr² + Δc²). Implement `euclidean(a, b)`.

ch10.title: Chebyshev Heuristic (8-connected)
ch10.prompt: For an 8-connected grid, h = max(|Δr|, |Δc|). Implement `chebyshev(a, b)`.
ch10.solution:
def chebyshev(a, b):
    return max(abs(a[0]-b[0]), abs(a[1]-b[1]))

ch11.title: PID Control Law
ch11.prompt: u = Kp·e + Ki·∫e + Kd·ė. Implement `pid(Kp, Ki, Kd, e, edot, eint)`.
ch11.starter:
def pid(Kp, Ki, Kd, e, edot, eint):
    pass
ch11.solution:
def pid(Kp, Ki, Kd, e, edot, eint):
    return Kp*e + Ki*eint + Kd*edot

ch11.title: PD Set-Point Control
ch11.prompt:
PD law toward a desired state: u = Kp·(θd − θ) + Kd·(θ̇d − θ̇).

Implement `pd_control(Kp, Kd, th, thd, th_d, thd_d)` (th_d, thd_d are desired).
ch11.starter:
def pd_control(Kp, Kd, th, thd, th_d, thd_d):
    pass
ch11.solution:
def pd_control(Kp, Kd, th, thd, th_d, thd_d):
    return Kp*(th_d - th) + Kd*(thd_d - thd)

ch11.title: PD Stability Condition
ch11.prompt: θ̈ₑ + Kd·θ̇ₑ + Kp·θₑ = 0 is stable iff Kp>0 and Kd>0. Implement `stable(Kp, Kd)` → bool.
ch11.solution:
def stable(Kp, Kd):
    return Kp > 0 and Kd > 0

ch11.title: Classify Damping
ch11.prompt:
Given damping ratio ζ, classify the second-order error response: ζ>1 'overdamped', ζ=1 'critically damped', 0<ζ<1 'underdamped'.

Implement `damping_type(zeta)` returning the exact string.

ch12.title: Friction Cone Half-Angle
Inside the Friction Cone?
ch12.prompt: No slip when |ft| ≤ μ·fn (fn ≥ 0). Implement `in_friction_cone(mu, fn, ft)` → bool.
ch12.solution:
def in_friction_cone(mu, fn, ft):
    return abs(ft) <= mu*fn

ch12.title: Min Contacts for Form Closure
ch12.prompt:
First-order form closure needs at least 4 frictionless point contacts in the plane, 7 in space.

Implement `min_contacts_form_closure(planar)` (bool → int).
ch12.solution:
def min_contacts_form_closure(planar):
    return 4 if planar else 7

ch12.title: Planar Contact Wrench
ch12.prompt:
A planar force (fx,fy) applied at point (px,py) produces wrench [m, fx, fy] with moment m = px·fy − py·fx.

Implement `contact_wrench(fx, fy, px, py)`.
ch12.starter:
def contact_wrench(fx, fy, px, py):
    pass
ch12.solution:
def contact_wrench(fx, fy, px, py):
    return [px*fy - py*fx, fx, fy]

ch13.title: Differential-Drive Chassis Twist
ch13.prompt: ω = r(uR−uL)/L, vx = r(uR+uL)/2, vy = 0. Implement `diff_drive(r, L, uL, uR)` → [ω, vx, vy].
ch13.starter:
def diff_drive(r, L, uL, uR):
    pass
ch13.solution:
def diff_drive(r, L, uL, uR):
    return [r*(uR-uL)/L, r*(uR+uL)/2, 0]

ch13.title: Diff-Drive Inverse: Wheel Speeds
ch13.prompt:
Invert the diff-drive map: given chassis (v, ω) find wheel speeds uL=(2v−ωL)/(2r), uR=(2v+ωL)/(2r).

Implement `wheel_speeds_diff(v, w, r, L)` → [uL, uR].
ch13.starter:
def wheel_speeds_diff(v, w, r, L):
    pass
ch13.solution:
def wheel_speeds_diff(v, w, r, L):
    return [(2*v - w*L)/(2*r), (2*v + w*L)/(2*r)]

ch13.title: Unicycle Odometry Step
ch13.prompt:
Integrate one step (Euler): x' = x + v·cosφ·dt, y' = y + v·sinφ·dt, φ' = φ + ω·dt.

Implement `unicycle_step(x, y, phi, v, w, dt)` → [x, y, phi].
